tokens: I'll be going about the same heading, Joe.
I'll just keep this heading and with any luck at all, you might be able to point out the front and take a ride with us.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, on the far side of the rille there, Dave, I sure see layering, over at 1 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, let's get up here first.
(Pause) See if we can find one of the Twins here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I get the feeling like we're coming up the real ridge line, don't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I can see one of the Twins over there at (garbled):30.
There's some good blocks down there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Good places all along here to sample large blocks on this side of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like the blocks there are almost in position.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And we are on The Terrace.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We could probably drive down there, though.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we can drive over, straight ahead, and stay on a fairly level contour.
We don't want to go down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, you want to just drive (garbled; heavy static)
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Static) I think that's Rim Crater there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Think I'm going to park right up here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This should be right where we park.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That would be a be a good picture for Houston.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Dave, when you climb off, could you...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Lost under Joe) TV by the AGC.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're off and stopped; and let me get on with the tasks here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We're at a nice place to stop and we're...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and, Jim, you may want to use Dave's camera to record this (station) on film, while Dave uses...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, you must have dropped comm there, Joe.
That's what we're in the process of doing here.
After I dust your eye off.
(Pause as Dave does the dusting) How's that, Joe?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Fendell) Attaboy; swing it around there, and you're going to see a spectacular place.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, if you're still there, we'd take some Rover readouts, but it's not crucial.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: The voltages (are) 68, 68; battery temperatures, 101 and about 110; and motor temps are off-scale, low.
The bearing is 088; the range is 1.8; distance, 2.5.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can see from up at the top of the rille down, there's debris all the way.
And, it looks like some outcrops directly at about 11 o'clock to the Sun line.
About 5 percent of the rille wall (height), with a vertical face on it.
And, within the vertical face, I can see other small lineations, horizontal about maybe 10 percent of that unit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And that unit outcrops (at various places) along the rille.
It's about 10 percent from the top, and it's somewhat irregular; but it looks to be a continuous layer.
It may be portions of (mare basalt) flows, but they're generally at about the 10-percent level.
I can see another one at about 12 o'clock to the Sun line, which is somewhat thinner, maybe 5 percent of the total depth of the rille.
However, it has a more-well-defined internal layering of about 10 percent of its thickness.
I can see maybe 10 very well-defined layers within that unit.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As I go down the rille, below this upper layer at 10 percent, there seems to be mostly debris in the order of large angular fragments, maybe the largest being like 5 percent of the total depth of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And then they gradually break on down to very small fragments and a talus slope.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I see no significant collection of talus at any level.
It seems to be fairly uniformly distributed in patches all the way down, to as far as I can see, to the bottom of the rille.
In looking on to my 12:30 to 1 o'clock on up the rille...And, I guess we'll get a little closer, when we get down to sampling it down there.
Why, it looks very much the same.
Outcrops of this one unit, irregularly spaced, discontinuous, but along the general 10 percent from the top line; with the talus sliding down into the bottom of the rille.
I see no differences in color.
However, the vertical section of the unit, which is exposed, looks to be somewhat lighter in gray.
The blocks, which have fallen down into the talus, seem to have a more tan or different tone of gray color to them.
Sort of like the fresh vertical section was more recently exposed.
Let me let you digest that for a minute, and let me take a bunch of 500's.
I'll get you the vertical and the horizontal and, boy, there's lots of things to shoot at over there.
(Pause) Hey, Jim, where'd you take the pan?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Where there's a little circle (of disturbed soil) on the ground.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: First, I'll get you a horizontal strip along the two outcrops.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I just sampled a fragment here with a great number of vesicles; vesicles about 2 millimeters in diameter.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, I'll get you a horizontal strip of the...I guess I have to say there is more accumulation of talus at about the 60-percent-from-the-top level, that I can see, Joe.
If I think about it for a minute, I can see more talus accumulation there, so that there might be some change in slope, but it's not apparent by looking at the slopes.
And I'll get you a horizontal strip there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Reacting to the TV view) Isn't that something?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And down about, oh, 20 feet from where Dave's taking a picture, there's a block about 2 feet; it's almost rectangular and the top surface is covered with large vesicles.
It almost looks like a contact there between that thin layer of vesicles and a rock that's a little lighter in color with fewer vesicles.
In fact, there's really horizontal orientation of the vesicles in this one.
I'll take a close-up on it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, and there's (what) looks like a crater in the far wall, at about 9 o'clock to the Sun line.
It's a round, circular depression, almost doesn't look like the kind of crater that would occur in a slope like that.
There's no buildup at the bottom.
The rim seems to be fairly parallel to the slope of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Horizontal strip above it, which should take in the upper 10 percent (of the rille wall).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me know if I get in your way, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You know, looking to the south, along the rim, along this side of the rille.
Dave, did you comment on that horizontal bedding that's probably, oh, at least 1 kilometer south us?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: No, I didn't even look on this side, to tell you the truth, Jim.
(Brief pause) Oh, I can see a couple of outcrops on the far side, which look like they might be in place at about the 40-percent level of the rille.
Very large boulders with fractures in them, rounded.
It's hard to tell whether they're really in place, but they may be in place covered by talus.
Let's see if there's any continuity to it.
I can see some suggestions of continuity there.
Jim, look about...Well, it looks like that the talus of fragments and fines is covering another layer.
Or a suggestion of continuity of outcrops, which are rounded, at about the 40- to 50-percent level down.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I see what you mean.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Jim, not having heard Joe yet) Yeah, it is.
Okay, let's summarize real...(Hearing Joe) Oh, frame number; yeah.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I guess that'll do it for here.
To summarize here, I think we see from the top to the bottom, one distinct layer about 10 percent, which has multilayers within it.
And, another at about 40 percent (down), which looks like a solid unit of a somewhat tanner hard rock, but it's covered with fines and talus.
And, we haven't seen to the bottom; I think we'll get a chance to look further down...Ohh!
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Ease that (500-mm camera) up for me?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just a minute, let me get it down a little lower.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Pretty durable little fellow (meaning the camera and lens).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...you might check the lens; and, if it looks reasonably clean, see if you could get the bit of outcrop on the near side to the south.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And I'll brush it off anyway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Joe, I'm documenting another rock here that looks fairly representative of what's on the surface here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I got the stop down to eight.
I'll see what I can do down...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: See what I was talking about down there, Dave?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Some...Somewhat...Looks like it might be dipping very slightly to the east.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can see the exposed upper surface of that layer.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Noticing Dave's turn toward Trophy Point) If you're going to shoot some more, I'll go out and get some more rocks there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: But I think we ought to, maybe, either move downslope...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, let's go down there and sample.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This time I'll look and make sure I don't fall over some silly rock.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Why don't you head down, I'll be right behind you.
I've got one more here I want to gather.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Except I don't have a (Hasselblad) camera, so I can't do anything (in terms of documentation).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Pick out one, and I'll come down and document it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's...We'll just ease down to this outcrop here in front of us.
It's good solid firm ground here, Joe.
(Pause) And I'll see how it is going back up.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pointing off-camera to the right) Oh, did you...Oh, yeah, you looked at the big one there that has the...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, I took some close-ups of that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You should see the vesicles in there and the alignment...the orientation of the vesicles.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I can almost see...(Pause) (Garbled) Looks like little pits in the dirt.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...is that a reasonable area for a rake sample, do you think?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Okay, maybe that's the quick way to get a bunch of them.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: It'll be a good place for one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: We can take the rake sample near the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Here's some...(Pause) Oh, well, we got to get some of that.
It looks like a basalt, and I think I see plage in it.
(We'll) have to break a chip off from one of those.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's sample this out...See these frags right on the surface here?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: This like it came from somewhere.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Pick one and I'll take the pictures.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We'll do that one right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get a fragment off it, you mean?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Just this side of the gnomon?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This'll be probably our last documented sample that we'll have time for.
We're going to ask you to move on back to the Rover when you're finished here for a rake sample.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And we're looking at about 10 to 15 more minutes at this stop.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, I thought that was...That's a big rock there!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Did you see where that frag went?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Keep your eye on what I got here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Are we going to have time to go down and sample the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Stand by for an answer on that, Jim.
And in the meantime, we have a truly magnificent view of the two of you here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Pause) Joe, this is a...It's a tan, fine-grained crystalline rock.
I've got to say that, because it's got up to 2-millimeter laths of plage in it, randomly oriented.
And the matrix is a sort of light gray to tan.
It's a very well indurated (that is, hard) rock.
On the outside, I've got nice glass-filled tip, and some other pits in it.
It's sure solid; and sure looks crystalline.
It came from this large block over here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You want to put some of those other fragments that are...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't I just get some of the other frags right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave and Jim, we've got an answer on your question when you're ready.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: If you think you can get pieces of true bedrock, we'll be willing to give up mare sampling station on the way back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Well, I think we can get...I think right...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, to the north of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pointing west and a little north) Right over there I think...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It's just too massive not to be.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Here let me hold that frag.
Get a scoop of the fines, and then we'll put the other frag in the bag, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That chip off the old boulder there was 275.
(Handing the bag to Jim) Why don't you zip this one.
And I'll get...Oh, man; just having bags...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let me get a bag off of you there.
(Pause as Dave gets a bag) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And out of sheer curiosity, how far back from what you would call the edge of the rille are the two of you standing now?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I don't know...Well, about 50 meters from where I guess we'd say we see real outcrop.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: How far back from the lip of the rille do you think you're probably standing?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Can't tell, I can't see the lip of the rille.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like you are standing on the edge of a precipice on TV; that's why we're asking.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, oh, oh, gosh, no, Joe.
It slopes right on down here.
It's just a little inflection here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get a little closer, so you get that big chip out of there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's go down and get a chunk of the bedrock here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, you're getting the bedrock here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) I thought you were going to press on to the north.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, he said go get the bedrock, and I think we ought to try and get it if we can.
Because this sure looks like a bedrock to me.
I looked down the rille to the south, and it's just one great big massive layer of the same kind of fragmental debris on the order of meters (in size).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, but the thing that bothers me, Dave, is look to the north there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And there's a flat area there, it looks like it might be the top of the bedrock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And those blocks seem to be slightly different.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I see what you are talking about.
Come on down here and let's get a frag off of one of these boulders and then we'll head on back to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Can you get the cross-Sun from over here, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Hey, Joe, these rounded fragments down here - on the order of meters in size - expose some very large, oh, 2 (to) 3 centimeter vesicles rather than the finer stuff that Jim saw back there before.
And I believe, when I take a chip out of this, we're going to find it's the same, kind-of-crystalline basalt.
And they're all...Well, they're subangular; looks like they've been weathered (by tiny impacts).
Fairly clean on the surface and all buried.
And I can look down to the south, and it's just a whole mass of great big boulders along the terrace here.
And there's another break-off down into the rille.
And I guess we're just about at the lip.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; I got them all located.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause as Dave stares at the ground) Thought I had them located.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; this looks like a darker, fine-grained, black, vesicular basalt, with vesicles on the order of millimeters (which are) non-uniformly distributed.
There are laths of plagioclase about 3 millimeters long, and it may be a half a millimeter wide, randomly-oriented throughout.
And that's about the only other mineral I see.
And that...Did you get the number on that, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's one other frag down here that fell - that I got.
Let me get a couple of rounded ones here, too, that are just on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I can't tell what that is, but we'll put it in anyway, as representative of surface material; at least the fragmental surface.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay; why don't you zip that one?
Here let me zip it, and you can take the "after" picture, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Boy, it hurts not to have two cameras.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Cover up what I just did.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Dave, when you finish this, we'd like you to move back towards the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, I think we ought to do that.
Need to stick the hammer on you.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I just wonder if that rock to the north...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Maybe we could stop there for the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Okay, let's head back to the Rover.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and we want a rake sample near the Rover...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Jim) Did you take a location shot down to the south, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Take a location shot down there...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Get it at infinity; f/8 at infinity and maybe take another one up here, another 15 or 20 meters or so.
Get a good stereo down to the south.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (To Houston) Hold until we get back to the Rover to talk about the rake sample, Joe.
Because you'll just have to tell us again.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You can see a boulder exposed to the surface here, which has got layering within it.
It's been weathered away, apparently, and just the surface top is exposed; but the boulder must be, oh, about a meter long with 2- to 3-inch layers in it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Would you get a picture of that where I stopped, Jim, just a quickie cross-Sun?
See where that thing is exposed there?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think a cross-Sun stereo would be neat right there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: As a matter of fact, I'll drop the gnomon; that'll tell them what it was.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just to get a real quick picture.
Oh, you're kicking up white albedo.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That's the only place I've seen it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Too bad we don't have time to pick some up, but we'll get probably pieces (in the rake sample).
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Say again your rake requirements, if they're different from the others?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No different, Dave, just a few frags using the rake...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, I think there's probably good statistical samples to be had here.
Hey, why don't you hand me the camera?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And you can take the pictures...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Take it off, it'd be faster.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Why don't we take a few steps down, Jim?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: So we get where there's more frags down here, I think.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Just afraid they'll be too large down there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (If I don't) fall down the hill.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Pause) Just a minute, let me get the down-Sun here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And I'll pick us out a route to go when we leave here.
Get up to North Twin, and there's a nice outcrop up there, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Kicking dirt onto the rake site) Ooop, oh.
Gee, I just walked right into your area.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Looks like some laths, vesicular basalt, nonvesicular basalt.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, I'll try to avoid that larger one there.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And I think I kicked up some more light-colored albedo.
I think, if we have some time when you get through, we ought to make a quick trench, here, maybe.
It looks like maybe the upper couple of inches might be the dark gray and below it the very light gray albedo.
(Pause as Jim pours) Okay; there's two swaths (each) about a meter long and one rake-width wide.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And are those frags (as opposed to clods)?
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Jim's getting down about, oh, I'd say...(Answering Joe) Yeah.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: That I have in my hand?
He's getting about, oh, 8 to 10 in each one, and it seems like there's a fair variety in there.

tokens: (To Jim) Hey, do it once...Let me move the gnomon here.
Take another swath over here so...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Garbled) so I can take two swaths, if you want.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I think we're getting something here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: It looks like you're getting a good 2 to 3 inches down, as you rake through there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Must be hung up on a large one here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Hey, Joe, how about a quick single core here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Or maybe even a double core.
We think you can probably drive two of them.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I think we probably can, too.
I was just giving you a little bait there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: A piece of cake compared to that drill.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Even I can agree with that, Joe.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think that's probably the best one they'll see.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: There's a big rock in there, huh?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Let's get a whole bag full.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think this is a Number One kind, Joe.
(To Jim) Hang on to that, or you can put it in my pack while I zip this.

tokens: Did you give him the number for this one?

tokens: Here, I'll hand you this one; the other one, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Changing his mind) No, I'll get yours (garbled).

tokens: Okay, let me get the ("after") pictures.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Listen, I'll go up (to the Rover) and take the rake off.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Get us set up for the core.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, Joe, you can remember on this particular sample that I moved the gnomon about 2 feet, so Jim could get (counting swaths) 1, 2, 3, 4...I guess we got 1, 2, 3, 4, 5 swaths there.

tokens: But you know, I don't know, a double core...We may find ourselves driving into bedrock if we're not careful.

tokens: (At the back of the Rover) Yeah, I'm afraid of that.

tokens: There's a nice crater here, on the edge.

tokens: And, I see some white-colored albedo near the...(Stops to listen)

tokens: I guess we'd prefer it away from the rim.

tokens: And there's light-colored albedo (lost under Joe) by the lower side of the...(Stops to listen)

tokens: I'll grab the core while you take the pictures.

tokens: I'll take the pictures, and then I'll get you.

tokens: And it's a little...Maybe we can do it here.

tokens: Jim, did you call the number?

tokens: You know, that light-colored albedo normally occurs on the lower...lower rim or the downhill rim.

tokens: I was just going to do that.

tokens: Let me get ready to take your picture.

tokens: It feels like it's hung up on a rock.

tokens: You're getting a couple inches a stroke.

tokens: (I) notice when you hit it, the whole ground around it raises up, for about an inch away from the core.

tokens: I'll give you a double core on that.

tokens: You can check off the double core square...

tokens: Go ahead and pull it up.

tokens: (To Houston) I like the ones where I can push them in, Joe.

tokens: I think you got a good one.

tokens: Yeah, we went right through a rock.

tokens: No wonder it was hard pounding.

tokens: Might clean it off so you get a good seal on the cap, Jim.

tokens: If you could clean that off.

tokens: I think we got a good seal.

tokens: Take off your end and I'll ram it.

tokens: Let me try holding this end and let's see

tokens: I can't get that cap on any more than that.

tokens: Hey, Joe, that's a good double core!

tokens: Okay, because I think you did...

tokens: Dave, while you're getting loaded up there...
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: ...our next request is two undocumented 6-inch blocks, and then we'll want you on the Rover driving north.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: You get one and I'll get one.

tokens: Hey, here's a good vesicular one.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...I don't know if we want to be too selective here if we're supposed to move on.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Want me to come down, Dave?

tokens: A little bigger than 6 inches, but it was neat looking.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: (Chuckling) Well, that makes up for it.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I'll probably move that (broken LMP) camera over onto your side, Dave, so I can just put rocks on that side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Can't put my camera over there, huh?

tokens: ...we want you to climb aboard now and head north about .3 or .4 clicks by the easiest route, and we'll pick up the stereo pan with the big camera.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And, Davy, we suggest you take those big camera pictures of the same items you photographed before, and, Jim, you can get the (70-mm) pan (with the CDR camera).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (Probably reaching across the seats) Here, let me just give you my camera now, Jim.
120 on the frames on my camera, (magazine) Sierra.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we also need the TV camera turned off at this time, and see if you could brush the top of the camera with your glove, please.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: (To Joe) (Flight Director) Gerry (Griffin)'s quick today on you, isn't he?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: No, you get in and then I can brush and tie you in, then.

tokens: We're not going that far, Dave.

tokens: Yeah, I want you tied in.

tokens: Joe, the top of the (TV) camera has virtually no dust on it whatsoever.

transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think we're just getting to a high Sun angle.

tokens: Because I notice I feel a little warmer today than I did yesterday.
